intent: On router 9, add an IPv6 route to 3000::/64 through interface 9-eth0 and enable IPv6 forwarding.
Find the neighbor of interface 0-eth2 and configure a static route on router 0 to 172.16.9.0/24 via that neighbor's IP.
On router 7, configure a static route to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.6.1 with administrative distance 50.
Create a floating default route on router 0 via 10.0.1.2 with distance 250.
Configure on router 4 a static route to 172.16.8.0/24 via directly connected neighbor 8.
On router 1, add a route to 172.16.6.0/24 via router 3.
Configure router 5 to reach 172.16.7.0/24 using next-hop 10.0.5.1.
On router 2, install a default route via router 5.
Remove all static routes on router 7 that point to prefix 172.16.0.0/24.
Replace the default route on router 0 so traffic exits through router 9.
Enable IP forwarding on router 7 and set the MTU of interface 7-eth1 to 9000 bytes.
On router 9, assign the IPv6 address 2001:db8:1::1/64 to interface 9-eth0.
Set interface 4-eth2 MTU to 1600 bytes.
Shutdown interface 3-eth1 and then bring it back up.
Enable IPv6 forwarding on router 2.
Assign IPv6 address 2001:db8:2::1/64 to interface 2-eth1.
Configure interface 0-eth3 description as LAN_USERS.
Bring down interface 8-eth0.
Bring up interface 8-eth0.
Set MTU 9216 on interface 5-eth1 and enable IP forwarding on router 5.
Configure a static ARP entry on router 0 mapping 10.0.0.2 to MAC address 02:00:00:00:00:02.

expected_commands.command: ip neigh add 10.0.0.2 lladdr 02:00:00:00:00:02 dev 0-eth0 nud permanent

intent: On router 7, create a static ARP entry for 10.0.9.2 with MAC 02:00:00:00:09:02.

expected_commands.command: ip neigh add 10.0.9.2 lladdr 02:00:00:00:09:02 dev 7-eth1 nud permanent

intent: Delete the static ARP entry on router 0 for neighbor 10.0.0.2 on interface 0-eth0.
notes: Based on the topology, router 0 interface 0-eth0 peers with router 4 interface 4-eth0, whose IP is 10.0.0.2. The intent is made specific instead of claiming all static ARP entries.

intent: Find the neighbor connected to 4-eth0 and configure a static ARP entry for its IP address.
notes: Topology resolves the neighbor as router 0 / 0-eth0 with IP 10.0.0.1, but it does not provide the MAC address required for a static ARP entry. Runtime discovery or human input is required.

intent: Configure a static route on router 1 to reach the LAN subnet of router 6 using the IP of the directly connected neighbor on the path from router 1 to router 6.
Block inbound SSH traffic on interface 0-eth3.
Allow HTTP traffic outbound on interface 0-eth3 and deny everything else.
Block ICMP traffic from 172.16.7.0/24 on router 2.
Permit only TCP port 443 inbound on interface 9-eth2.
Deny traffic from host 172.16.4.10 reaching router 0 LAN.
Create ACL on router 1 allowing SSH from 172.16.1.10 only.
Apply an ACL on router 5 blocking telnet traffic outbound.
Permit ICMP echo requests on router 8 LAN interface.
Block UDP traffic outbound on interface 7-eth2.
Allow DNS traffic and block all other UDP on router 3.
Apply a rate limit of 100Mbps on interface 0-eth2 for outbound TCP traffic.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 0-eth2 root handle 1: htb default 10
tc class add dev 0-eth2 parent 1: classid 1:1 htb rate 100mbit
tc filter add dev 0-eth2 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 6 0xff flowid 1:1

intent: Limit outbound traffic on 7-eth1 to 50Mbps.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 7-eth1 root handle 1: htb default 10
tc class add dev 7-eth1 parent 1: classid 1:1 htb rate 50mbit
tc filter add dev 7-eth1 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip dst 0.0.0.0/0 flowid 1:1

intent: Police inbound traffic on 4-eth3 to 20Mbps.

expected_commands.command: tc filter add dev 4-eth3 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip src 0.0.0.0/0 police rate 20mbit burst 10k drop flowid :1

intent: Apply priority treatment for SSH traffic on interface 5-eth2.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 5-eth2 root handle 1: prio bands 3
tc filter add dev 5-eth2 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip dport 22 0xffff flowid 1:1

intent: Limit UDP traffic on router 9 LAN interface to 10Mbps.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 9-eth2 root handle 1: htb default 10
tc class add dev 9-eth2 parent 1: classid 1:1 htb rate 10mbit
tc filter add dev 9-eth2 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 17 0xff flowid 1:1

intent: Configure a BGP peer-group named IBGP on router 0, set remote-as 65000, and bind neighbor 10.0.1.2 to it.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor IBGP peer-group' -c 'neighbor IBGP remote-as 65000'

intent: On router 5, create BGP process 65000 and peer with router 0.
Configure router 2 with BGP ASN 65100 and advertise network 172.16.2.0/24.
Create peer-group EDGE on router 4 with remote-as 65200 and attach neighbor 10.0.8.2.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65200' -c 'neighbor EDGE peer-group' -c 'neighbor EDGE remote-as 65200'

intent: Remove BGP neighbor 10.0.1.2 from router 0.
Enable IP forwarding on router 9, assign IPv6 2001:db8:9::1/64 to 9-eth1, and add default IPv6 route via 9-eth0.
On router 0, create a backup route to 172.16.8.0/24 via router 4 with distance 200 and primary via router 9 with distance 10.
Block SSH from 172.16.7.0/24 on router 0 and rate-limit HTTP traffic on 0-eth3 to 30Mbps.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 0-eth3 root handle 1: htb default 10
tc class add dev 0-eth3 parent 1: classid 1:1 htb rate 30mbit
tc filter add dev 0-eth3 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip dport 80 0xffff flowid 1:1

intent: Find the neighbor on interface 2-eth1, then configure a route from router 2 to 172.16.9.0/24 through that neighbor.
Configure router 1 to advertise 172.16.1.0/24 in BGP, enable forwarding, and set MTU 2000 on 1-eth1.
Set MED to 100 for BGP neighbor 10.0.2.2 and enable AS-path multipath on router 0.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.2.2 route-map SET_MED out'
vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax'

intent: Configure a static route on router 3 to 172.16.4.0/24 via its directly connected neighbor router 1.
notes: Router 3 is directly connected to router 1 over 10.0.3.0/30; router 1 also connects toward router 4 over 10.0.4.0/30. The previous next-hop 10.0.4.2 was not directly reachable from router 3.

intent: Configure BGP local-preference to 200 for peer-group EDGE on router 4.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65200' -c 'neighbor EDGE route-map SET_LOCALPREF in'

intent: Bring down interface 1-eth1 and remove its IP address.
Disable ARP on interface 0-eth2 of router 0.

expected_commands.command: ip link set dev 0-eth2 arp off

intent: Enable IPv4 forwarding globally on router 7.
Administratively shut down interface 8-eth0 on router 8.
Bind neighbor 10.0.8.2 to the BGP peer-group EDGE on router 4.
Configure a static route on router 1 to reach the LAN subnet of router 5, using the IP of the neighbor connected to 1-eth1 as the gateway.
Find the router connected to interface 0-eth1, and configure a static route on that remote router back to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.1.1.
Apply a 50Mbps rate limit for inbound UDP traffic on the specific interface of router 4 that connects to router 0.

expected_commands.command: tc filter add dev 4-eth0 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 17 0xff police rate 50mbit burst 10k drop flowid :1

intent: On router 2, create a BGP peer-group named TRANSIT, set remote-as 64512, and set the BGP authentication password to 'SeCrEt' for this group.
notes: Intentionally retained as a negative catalog-coverage case: BGP MD5 authentication password configuration is not supported in the current operation catalog, so no executable command is expected.

intent: Set the local-preference to 150 for peer-group IBGP on router 0, and configure MED to 50 for neighbor 10.0.2.2.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor IBGP route-map SET_LP in'
vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.2.2 route-map SET_MED_50 out'

intent: Apply a route-map named BLOCK_DEFAULT in the inbound direction for BGP neighbor 10.0.1.2 on router 0.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.1.2 route-map BLOCK_DEFAULT in'

intent: Remove the BGP peer-group EDGE from router 4 and delete all associated static routes pointing to 172.16.1.0/24.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65200' -c 'no neighbor EDGE peer-group'

intent: Add a static route on router 6 to 172.16.8.0/24 via its directly connected neighbor router 3.
notes: Router 6 has only one router-facing interface in the topology: 6-eth0 peers with 3-eth1, whose IP is 10.0.7.1. The previous primary/backup version required next-hops that were not valid directly connected neighbors of router 6.

intent: Disable ARP, shut down interface 7-eth0, and remove its IPv4 address on router 7.

expected_commands.command: ip link set dev 7-eth0 arp off

intent: Enable dual-stack forwarding on router 3, then assign IPv4 10.0.3.1/30 and IPv6 2001:db8:3::1/64 to interface 3-eth0.
Change the IP address of 1-eth0 on router 1 to 10.0.10.1/30 and ensure the interface is administratively up.
Configure a blackhole route on router 9 for the prefix 10.100.0.0/16 by pointing it to the Null0 interface.
Limit inbound ICMP traffic to 5Mbps on interface 2-eth0 of router 2.

expected_commands.command: tc filter add dev 2-eth0 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 1 0xff police rate 5mbit burst 10k drop flowid :1

intent: Delete the static ARP entry for 10.0.0.2 on router 0, and clear the description of interface 0-eth0.
Disable IPv6 forwarding globally on router 4, but ensure IPv4 forwarding remains enabled.
Assign the IP address 10.255.255.1/32 to the loopback interface on router 1.
Set the description of interface 0-eth2 on router 0 to TO_ROUTER_9 and set the description of 9-eth0 on router 9 to TO_ROUTER_0.
Set the OSPF cost of interface 5-eth0 on router 5 to 100 and configure its description as PRIMARY_LINK.
Enable Jumbo frames by setting the MTU to 9216 on interface 8-eth0 of router 8, and restart the interface by bringing it down then up.
Find the IPv4 network CIDR assigned to interface 3-eth1 and configure a static route on router 0 pointing to that network via 10.0.1.2.
Bring interface 3-eth1 administratively up and configure its description as BACKBONE_TO_6.
On router 9, assign the IPv4 address 172.16.9.254/24 to interface 9-eth2.
